title: cynoglossum ochroleucum
intro: Cynoglossum ochroleucum, also known as the white-tinged tonguewort or white-hooded gypsy flower, is a perennial plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae) native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and acting as an antispasmodic. In herbal preparations, Cynoglossum ochroleucum can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
